item.title: No matter what people might think, what might have …
item.description: [Source: Hoops Hype] Where do you come out on that subject? LeBron or Mike?” Jerry Stackhouse: “I mean, from some skill standpoints, he’s probably better. He’s a better ball handler than Michael Jordan was. I think he’s a better facilitator than Michael Jordan was. Was he a better scorer than Michael Jordan? No. Was he a better clutch performer? No. Michael Jordan went to the Finals six times, won six championships, six Finals MVPs. So, to me, he’s always going to be the GOAT in my book. No matt

item.title: “I doubt [Washington] decided who they want, just like …
item.description: Ainge is operating under the assumption that everything is on the table, including Dybantsa. “I doubt [Washington] decided who they want, just like we haven’t decided who we want,” Ainge said. “We will figure out who is one and two on our board and be ready. There are at least two good players in this draft.”

item.title: Wizards would consider trading down
item.description: And there just might be one. Winger told me directly that the Wizards will at least consider trading down. He insisted that this is "not a savior moment" for Washington given that the franchise just traded for two former All-Stars in Trae Young and Anthony Davis on top of the slew of recent lottery picks it already has accumulated. He added that, in accordance with Wizards general manager Will Dawkins' prospect evaluations, Washington will not rule out a move downward if Dawkins determines there

item.title: Wizards, Jazz, Grizzlies and Bulls make the top four in the draft lottery
item.description: Josh Robbins: The Wizards, Jazz, Grizzlies and Bulls are the teams that will draft somewhere in the top four.

item.title: "I've always had to play in a pair of Hanes crew socks …
item.description: JOHN WALL WAS sitting at the Adorn Bar in the Four Seasons. It was Sunday morning, and the No. 1 selection in the 2010 draft was picking at the bacon on his plate and explaining his superstitions: They were underneath his crisp navy blue and red Howard University Air Jordan 14 exclusives, he said. "I've always had to play in a pair of Hanes crew socks from Walmart, under my NBA socks," Wall told ESPN. "And they got to be ultra cushioned. I need them."

item.title: "Did you see that?" Wall asked, astounded at the …
item.description: Wall was at the restaurant in downtown Chicago representing his former team, the Washington Wizards, at perhaps the most pivotal point they've had in a generation -- since he arrived as a prized prospect 16 years ago. In between bites, Wall described his beloved good luck charm: a necklace his late mother wore. He last had it on Jan. 29, the night the Wizards celebrated his career. The clamp broke, however. Fortunately, he has a backup: a tattoo on his neck of his mother wearing the necklace. Ju

item.title: "This one feels like it's a lot more like, 'We need to …
item.description: ESPN was granted exclusive behind-the-scenes access to the Wizards throughout the draft lottery weekend, from Wall to their front office, seeking to capture a full view of an organization at its most tenuous time. Either they'd fall, yet again, delaying once more the NBA's most arduous rebuild, or they'd strike lottery gold and break in an instant a spell of misfortune that had lasted for decades. "This one feels like it's a lot more like, 'We need to do this!'" said Wall, who wanted a better re

item.title: "[Sunday,] I feel, was an incredible day of luck," …
item.description: Sunday's lottery luck provides the potential for a new, foundational pillar for a franchise used to disaster always being around the corner. "[Sunday,] I feel, was an incredible day of luck," Leonsis said. "If you watch 'Final Destination,' that's all I could think of was, 'Here I am. I'm going to end up in the hospital. I will have lost an eye and we'll pick fifth."

item.title: "I would say the Wizards can't have a bad day today."
item.description: The Wizards went into the day knowing they couldn't drop lower than fifth and were confident this draft -- headlined by college stars such as Kansas' Darryn Peterson, Duke's Cameron Boozer, North Carolina's Caleb Wilson and Arkansas' Darius Acuff Jr. -- would yield at least five players with All-Star ceilings. ESPN draft analyst Jeremy Woo projects BYU's AJ Dybantsa to be selected by Washington. "This draft is pretty deep," Dawkins said before the lottery started. "To know you can't fall further

item.title: AJ Dybantsa on going to the Wizards: "I play the game …
item.description: WizardsMuse: AJ Dybantsa on going to the Wizards: "I play the game the right way, I'm exciting and I fill seats" AURAAAAA THIS IS A WIZARD 🔥🔥

item.title: The other thing is that he has made it clear to the …
item.description: Shams Charania: But, yes, the big question is medical: what happened with the cramping? He’s telling teams, like he told Ramona Shelburne last week, that it was due to an excess of creatine intake. That’s how he has addressed it with teams. Moving forward, he believes he’s passed this issue. Washington and Utah will bring him in. They’ll have conversations. They’ll see his medicals and be able to review them. Two other things he has gotten across during this process: he wants to be the number on

item.title: While the connection there is logical, there's no …
item.description: While the connection there is logical, there's no indication that it will happen at this stage of the predraft process, with lottery teams having only recently been assigned their selections. At the moment, the thought of Utah trading up is purely speculative, as the Jazz are sitting in a strong position at No. 2 regardless: They can simply wait and see who Washington takes, something that is not being treated as a foregone conclusion.

item.title: Dybantsa has shaped up as the favorite at No. 1, but …
item.description: Dybantsa has shaped up as the favorite at No. 1, but that's not the consensus, with most around the NBA having seen Peterson as a strong candidate for that spot all season, and varying opinions on Boozer's and even Caleb Wilson's candidacies. The talent gap between that foursome, particularly Dybantsa and Peterson, might not ultimately be wide enough to trade up from No. 2 to 1 in a vacuum, regardless of which teams owned which picks.

item.title: Scout says AJ Dybantsa to Washington is 'absolutely not' a certainty
item.description: But we are uncovering contrarian opinions over the course of the last 48 hours at the NBA Draft Combine, when the front offices of all 30 NBA teams, players, agents, and media are essentially clustered in the same gym and hotel for the entirety of the day. First, as it relates to Washington, I asked a high-ranking scout on one of the other lottery teams if they considered Washington's selection of Dybantsa a certainty and if that would impact their own preparation. I got a more direct response

item.title: While there's more public speculation about other …
item.description: So what will the Jazz do at No. 2, especially considering Cameron Boozer's dad, Carlos Boozer, currently works for the organization in a scouting role? Most expect Kansas guard Darryn Peterson to be the Jazz's pick here. While there's more public speculation about other options, sources around the combine speak with almost the same level of confidence that Peterson goes No. 2 as they do Washington taking Dybantsa at No. 1.

item.title: Metta World Peace on Bam Adebayo’s 83-point game: ‘You can’t blame him for going for it, I would’ve done the same’
item.description: You were a teammate of Kobe Bryant, who had an 81-point performance prior to your arrival. Bam Adebayo recently surpassed this with an 83-point game. How impressive was that? Metta World Peace: I was very happy for him. I was super impressed. As I read more articles and heard a lot of the noise around it, it kind of diluted the emotion and excitement around it. It seemed like everyone was just talking about the free throws and the and-ones. He had 31 points in the first quarter. That’s crazy. At

item.title: The Sixers are going to be higher hiring a new head of …
item.description: Shams Charania: The Sixers are going to be higher hiring a new head of basketball operations, someone that will now carry it forwards from a leadership perspective, but that person will partner with Bob Myers, who's the four-time championship executive and architect of the Golden State Warriors, and now he's the president of sports for multiple teams on the portfolios of owners Josh Harris and David Blitzer. Remember, Bob Myers oversaw the hiring of Adam Peters with the Washington commanders. He

item.title: .@ramonashelburne thinks Darryn Peterson could go No. …
item.description: NBA on ESPN: .@ramonashelburne thinks Darryn Peterson could go No. 1 to the Wizards (via @RichEisenShow)

item.title: He turned to Michael Winger, then an executive with …
item.description: He turned to Michael Winger, then an executive with the Los Angeles Clippers who had never run his own shop. Winger recruited Will Dawkins, a young and sharp talent evaluator with whom Winger had worked in Oklahoma City, to be his general manager. The assignment: Tell Ted Leonsis the reality even if it might not be what he wanted to hear. “He said to me three or four times, ‘You know, this could take four or five years,’” Leonsis said of Winger. “And I said, ‘I totally understand. I’ve been thro

item.title: Michael Winger on why they traded for Trae Young: …
item.description: Wizards Film Room: More Michael Winger on why they traded for Trae Young: “He’s an absolute genius. He’s a savant with the basketball in his hands..up until that point, other players on the floor have had to create for themselves and others and we don’t have a pure natural playmaker. We struggle offensively to create because we don’t have someone as naturally gifted as he is at creating something. He’s an offensive engine, we lacked an offensive engine. We have great play finishers, we have grea

item.title: Russillo: “If you don’t trade him, I don’t think you …
item.description: Wizards Film Room: Michael Winger and @ryenarussillo talking about CJ McCollum: Russillo: “If you don’t trade him, I don’t think you end up with the #1 pick.” Winger: “No comment.” ????

item.title: Winger on who will play center between Sarr and AD: “I …
item.description: Wizards Film Room: Winger on who will play center between Sarr and AD: “I would imagine Alex. I would also submit that AD is a special talent and AD is a special guy and if AD says I need a break from the ‘5’ for a few minutes, BK [Brian Keefe] is going to give him a break from the ‘5’ for a few minutes so we will listen to Anthony. He has a very loud vote in what happens on the court.”

item.title: Michael Winger on Trae Young’s defense: “He’ll be the …
item.description: Michael Winger on Trae Young’s defense: “He’ll be the first to tell you he’s not a humongous athlete. He gives up size on the defensive end. But he’ll play with effort, he’ll play with intelligence…But he’s not a shot-blocker and guys could potentially bully him…but that’s okay because that puts more pressure on Alex and we don’t mind the pressure on Alex. That puts more pressure on Bilal, that’s fine, we want that pressure on those guys.”

item.title: Jazz have reached out to Wizards about trading up to take AJ Dybantsa No. 1 overall
item.description: AJ Dybantsa is the top prospect in the draft, according to ESPN’s Jeremy Woo. The Jazz have reached out to the Washington Wizards, who have the No. 1 pick, about potentially trading up to land the former BYU standout, a source said.

item.title: BYU forward AJ Dybantsa met with the Wizards in …
item.description: Marc J. Spears: BYU forward AJ Dybantsa met with the Wizards in Chicago during the NBA pre-draft camp. Washington is supposed to visit with “multiple players” in DC for the top pick, source days. Dybantsa also met with Utah, Chicago, Sacramento, Brooklyn, Atlanta, Dallas, Memphis & the Clippers.

item.title: Michigan’s Nimari Burnett has garnered high NBA …
item.description: Jake Weingarten: Michigan’s Nimari Burnett has garnered high NBA interest and has several NBA workouts, including the Washington Wizards and Charlotte Hornets next week, a source told @stockrisers. He will have several NBA workouts following that at the end of May and into June.

item.title: Wizards likely to deal, but not with Jazz
item.description: Brian Windhorst: The sentiment that I have heard is that Washington is sending out the message that they’re willing to deal, but if they do a deal, people think it will most likely not be with Utah. Of course, there’s a long time between now and then. Tim Bontemps: I feel pretty confident the Wizards are going to pick somebody at number one. Tim MacMahon: There were some—how do I want to say this—feathers ruffled in Salt Lake City about perceived shenanigans with Ace Bailey in the pre-draft proc

item.title: The only one that really took a secondary role but …
item.description: Kevin Garnett: LeBron James is still in the 20s averaging and he took a lesser role and like what's next for Bron, what do you think is next. Paul Pierce: Man when I start thinking about it I look at all the GOATs, Bron is in these conversation like, when I think of Jordan, when Jordan came to the Wizards he never took a secondary role. The only one that really took a secondary role but kind of still was first role was Kareem with Magic. Kobe never took a secondary role. Bird never like it's lik

item.title: Ten of the 13 executives and scouts we spoke with were …
item.description: Ten of the 13 executives and scouts we spoke with were willing to name the player they think Washington should draft at No. 1. Dybantsa received seven votes. Peterson garnered two votes. One person voted for Boozer. “Take AJ Dybantsa and sleep like a baby,” one of the seven respondents who voted for Dybantsa told us, saying that Dybantsa’s upside and floor are so high that he’s the safest pick in the draft. Dybantsa’s worst possible outcome, that respondent said, would be to have a career simila

item.title: “Utah has got assets and good players,” the respondent …
item.description: One front-office official we spoke with who voted for Dybantsa said that the Utah Jazz, who won the No. 2 pick in the lottery, would be an enticing potential trade partner. “Utah has got assets and good players,” the respondent said. “The question is: How good of a player could you get from the Jazz?”

item.title: NBA player anonymous poll names Memphis the least desired NBA destination
item.description: When players were asked which team would be at the top of their “no-trade” list, Memphis received more than three times as many votes as the second-place Washington Wizards. "I'll play anywhere, honestly,” one player explained. “But I would have to say Memphis is a place I don't want to live. So that would be the reason why I wouldn't want to play there.” Said another player: “It has nothing to do with the team. It's the location of the team."

item.title: Trae Young: Wemby may actually be an Alien
item.description: Trae Young: Wemby may actually be an Alien 👽

item.title: Rival teams don't view AJ Dybantsa as a lock to go first
item.description: Rival teams don't view Dybantsa as a lock to go first, however, as there has been no true consensus No. 1 among scouts all season. Darryn Peterson, Cam Boozer and Caleb Wilson are all expected to receive consideration as the Wizards go through their process. The fact that there is a consensus group of four has led some executives to view the top of this draft as more ripe for trades than in a typical year, but only if the top four teams see value in moving around within that tier. Any trade dyna

item.title: That means half the coaches in the league got hired …
item.description: Tim Reynolds: Brian Keefe will be no lower than the 15th longest-tenured coach going into next season. That means half the coaches in the league got hired after he got the Wizards job. Keefe got the job 1 year and 11 1/2 months ago.

item.title: I haven’t heard, I know there’s a lot of Caleb Wilson …
item.description: Will those three go in some order? Or are each of those three franchises having its own, internal debate? Wass: Yeah, definitely. One of the most popular questions I ask, of course — and I’ve been doing this for three, four months now — is, who’s your No. 1 guy? And I’ve heard three different answers. I haven’t heard, I know there’s a lot of Caleb Wilson buzz, I’ve never heard Caleb Wilson at one. I haven’t even heard Wilson two. I’ve heard Wilson three a couple of times, but you hear a lot of d

item.title: The star-studded training camp squad is highlighted by …
item.description: Head coach Frederic Fauthoux has named a 16-player roster for the senior national team of France heading into the upcoming July window of the FIBA Basketball World Cup 2027 European Qualifiers, as revealed by the French Federation of Basketball on Friday. The star-studded training camp squad is highlighted by the returns of veterans Rudy Gobert and Evan Fournier, alongside a strong NBA contingent featuring Bilal Coulibaly, Zaccharie Risacher, Alex Sarr, Guerschon Yabusele, and newcomer Maxime Ra

item.title: Additionally, the NBA has decided to start the clock …
item.description: Continuing the above example, this would mean that if Team B popped up first in the 2028 draft, that pick would automatically be moved to the second pick instead. Additionally, the NBA has decided to start the clock on these two-year and three-year streaks by looking back to the 2025 and 2026 drafts, according to league sources. In practice, this would mean if Washington wins the lottery again in 2027, it moves to second.

item.title: As for the Cavaliers, their partnership with The …
item.description: Since the 2023-24 season, Sotheby has worked with the league in hosting live NBA auctions (New York, Paris) and activations (Abu Dhabi). Sotheby’s sold Cooper Flagg’s debut jersey in February for a record-setting $1 million, which the league said eclipsed Victor Wembanyama’s previous record when his jersey sold for $762,000 in 2023. In March, Sotheby auctioned off various Scottie Pippen’s autographed memorabilia from the 1992 Olympics and from parts of his six NBA Finals appearances. The league

item.title: Marc Spears: I basically was told by a …
item.description: Marc Spears: I basically was told by a very good source close to that Washington situation that they're going to explore their options and I think that with some of the young talent that they have why not, right? the one thing I wonder with Giannis Antetokounmpo is like how much power does he have and where they trade him and does this relatively I guess new owner there really care? or does he have a relationship with Giannis where he's worried about his destination?

item.title: “I basically was told by a very good source close to …
item.description: The Miami Heat have been frequently floated as a team that would be interested in trading for Antetokounmpo, as have a number of others. However, the Washington Wizards have not been mentioned as a potential Giannis landing spot but maybe they should be, according to NBA insider Marc Spears. “I basically was told by a very good source close to that Washington situation that they're going to explore their options and I think that with some of the young talent that they have why not, right?” Spear

item.title: Jerry Stackhouse reveals why his trade to Michael Jordan's Wizards felt 'awkward'
item.description: Jerry Stackhouse: A lot of people make make a lot about our last interaction in Washington when I felt that I was kind of in the prime and he was at at the end of his career, but it's Michael Jordan. Michael Jordan no matter who he's on the floor with probably right now feels that he's the best player and the best option. So, I mean, I take no offense to that, but it was just didn't work out in his final year. He brought me there the year before, they had Rip Hamilton and he felt like I was furt

item.title: The NBA intends to implement these prohibitions “to …
item.description: The proposal distributed to teams late this week would not allow teams to win the No. 1 pick two years in a row, a restriction that would begin the clock with the 2026 draft. That means the Washington Wizards would not be able to pick first next year. The NBA would also bar teams from having a top-5 pick in three consecutive years. That rule will be backdated to the 2025 draft. Yahoo Sports was the first to detail this aspect. The NBA intends to implement these prohibitions “to promote a fair di

item.title: One NBA scout — from a lottery team outside the top 4 …
item.description: One NBA source said that the connection won’t mean much in the grand scheme of things and that it isn’t talked about in team meetings. And it may also be hard to move up in this draft, with two sources telling The Star there’s a heavy premium in acquiring a top-4 pick in what is viewed as a strong draft. One NBA scout — from a lottery team outside the top 4 — told The Star movement inside that top group could happen, but called it unlikely that any of those teams would trade a top-4 pick without

item.title: According to EPM, Towns had the best defensive season …
item.description: But is the newfound elite defensive performance sustainable? Listen to enough NBA podcasts, and you’ll frequently hear the take that the Knicks have two massive defensive holes to patch: Brunson at the point and Karl-Anthony Towns in the frontcourt. So even with a trio of very good “wing-stopper” defenders in OG Anunoby, Mikal Bridges and Josh Hart, they’re sort of treading water. The critique of Brunson is fair. He’s not a black hole like Trae Young out there, and he gets his share of steals, b

item.title: AJ Dybantsa says Trae Young will have to give up No. 3 …
item.description: NBA Central: AJ Dybantsa says Trae Young will have to give up No. 3 if he’s drafted by the Wizards ?? “If they draft me, I do need 3, Trae. If they draft me. We’re gonna see in like 5 weeks”

item.title: BYU forward AJ Dybantsa and Kansas guard Darryn …
item.description: BYU forward AJ Dybantsa and Kansas guard Darryn Peterson are the favorites to go first, though Duke’s Cam Boozer remains on the radar, per league sources. Wizards president Michael Winger has already said on the record that the front office will consider trading down to accumulate assets.

item.title: “I think I’d fit well there with their vision and …
item.description: Boozer met with the Wizards at the draft combine and had more nice things to say about possibly playing for them. “I think I’d fit well there with their vision and their plan for the future. Obviously, D.C. is a great city. We had a good conversation for sure,” he said.

item.title: Being told Ryan Richman, the former Wizards assistant, …
item.description: Jake Fischer: Being told Ryan Richman, the former Wizards assistant, has signed a deal to become the new head coach of Alvark Tokyo in the Japanese B.League Premier. After leaving Washington to lead SeaHorses Mikawa to three-straight playoff appearances, Richman was sought after by half a dozen teams and a few NBA assistant opportunities before agreeing with Alvark, widely regarded as Asia’s most coveted head coaching position.

item.title: My deepest condolences to the Family!
item.description: Trae Young: Being in the gym today that Stacey King helped build… then hearing the news My deepest condolences to the Family! Stacey King will never be forgotten

item.title: AJ Dybantsa remains Washington’s most likely target?
item.description: Sources say that Washington is doing its due diligence and that the Wizards haven’t made a decision yet on who they’ll draft No. 1. But sources across the league believe that Dybantsa remains Washington’s most likely target. He is seen as having extremely high upside while also possessing a high floor.

item.title: Trae Young getting three-year deal with Wizards?
item.description: Early projections on the likely ballpark for Young's new deal with the Wiz: $120 million over three seasons. Young has until June 23 — Night 1 of the draft — to activate or bypass the player option. He's eligible for a three-year extension worth nearly $160 million until June 30.

item.title: Wemby was asked about being a villain at MSG: “I …
item.description: Jared Weiss: Wemby was asked about being a villain at MSG: “I guess, I’m nowhere on Trae Young’s level though.”

item.title: "It won't take that long, especially with my …
item.description: AJ Dybantsa is watching a young San Antonio Spurs team built by high draft picks in the NBA Finals and sees a similar chance to have quick success as a pro. That would be good news for the Washington Wizards, who have the No. 1 pick this year and are expected to use it on the star freshman from BYU. "It won't take that long, especially with my adaptability and my work ethic," Dybantsa said Monday. "I think that will be, I'm not going to say easy, but pretty similar to what these guys do in terms

item.title: Though his teammate AJ Dybantsa has garnered more of …
item.description: Though his teammate AJ Dybantsa has garnered more of the headlines, as the projected No. 1 pick in the 2026 NBA draft, Keba Keita has consistently impressed NBA teams throughout the predraft process. “Keba is on the rise,” his agent, Keith Kreiter, said. “He’s been outplaying bigs who are projected to go in the first round. His physicality and ability to guard one through five makes him a no-brainer for the mid-to-late second round.” He’s worked out with the Utah Jazz, Washington Wizards, Charlo

item.title: Being in Atlanta was unbelievable. It's crazy how …
item.description: Trae Young: "I wouldn't trade any moment that I've had in the NBA back for anything. Being in Atlanta was unbelievable. It's crazy how eight years just went like this."

item.title: Trae Young: 'I'm not a villain outside of the court'
item.description: Trae Young on his persona as a villain: "You embrace it to an extent when you're on the court, you know what I'm saying? But if someone wants to get with it or get like an interaction out of me and try to be a villain outside of this, it's like I'm not a villain outside of the court, you know what I'm saying? And that's what I hope you get from me."

item.title: Trae Young: "I can play multiple ways.
item.description: Trae Young: "I can play multiple ways. A lot of people just see what I've done and me being ball-dominant. I can't wait to show other things. My boy Dejounte Murray, who's with me 24/7, he was my point guard for a lot of years and I was just a shooter. I didn't do nothing but just shoot the ball. So I can't wait to show that. But also, yes, you can win with a smaller guard, of course. It's been done before."

item.title: Trae Young on his relationship with Spike Lee: "Spike …
item.description: Trae Young on his relationship with Spike Lee: "Spike is as much as everybody says so many things, he's been such a good person to me and my family. He's giving me one of his books, I mean, has a handwritten letter in it that he wrote and gave it to me, I think it was after that series or at the end of that and I mean, just been nice to my pops and everything. So he's just been a great dude and that's the head of New York, you know what I'm saying?"

item.title: Trae Young: 'When I had my son, I started being afraid to fly'
item.description: Trae Young on balancing fatherhood and his NBA career: "When I had him, I started being I started being afraid to fly. So I started being afraid to fly and I was and I play in the NBA, like we fly every day. So it's like, how am I going to play if I can't fly? You know what I'm saying? But it was just more about the fact that I I didn't want to not be here for my kids. And so my dad, I mean, like I said, he's me and him, I don't butt heads with nobody else more on this world than my dad, but som

item.title: They gone move the bar
item.description: Jaylen Brown: They gone move the bar

item.title: The latest is located in New York Midtown, between …
item.description: Kaysuane has put together two murals during the Knicks’ spectacular run through the playoffs. The latest is located in New York Midtown, between 30th Street and 31st Street and 8th Avenue, depicting Jalen Brunson as the “King of the East,” with NBA guards Trae Young, Tyrese Maxey and James Harden bowing to the throne. Next to the image is a Larry O’Brien championship trophy with the Knicks logo drawn over it. A Game 3 loss in the NBA Finals hasn’t taken the spirit from Kaysuane or the New York C

item.title: Wizards to host potential No. 1 pick AJ Dybantsa on a visit
item.description: As the Washington Wizards prepare for a huge moment for the franchise — having the first pick in the 2026 NBA Draft on June 23 — multiple league sources told The Athletic that the team will host AJ Dybantsa, one of the prospects the team is considering to take at No. 1, for a visit with team officials in Washington in the next few days, perhaps as soon as Thursday.

item.title: A league source indicated this week that Washington …
item.description: A league source indicated this week that Washington has already met with North Carolina wing Caleb Wilson, another likely top four pick. Wilson did not work out for the team but met with team officials.

item.title: AJ Dybantsa: “If I go to DC, I’ll make the playoffs.”
item.description: Legion Hoops: AJ Dybantsa: “If I go to DC, I’ll make the playoffs.” (via @MrCongee)

item.title: Washington has narrowed its focus to Dybantsa and …
item.description: The Wizards have played their cards close to the vest over the past few weeks, taking their time with the process and evaluating all four candidates atop the draft. Washington has narrowed its focus to Dybantsa and Darryn Peterson, sources tell me and Charania.

item.title: Ace Dybantsa told me both meetings with the Wizards …
item.description: Krysten Peek: Ace Dybantsa told me both meetings with the Wizards and Jazz went well. AJ was in Washington Friday and Salt Lake City Saturday.

item.title: AJ Dybantsa has conducted formal visits with both the …
item.description: Shams Charania: Developments ahead of the June 23-24 NBA draft: BYU's AJ Dybantsa has conducted formal visits with both the Washington Wizards (No. 1) and Utah Jazz (No. 2) while Kansas' Darryn Peterson only visited the Wizards and does not plan to grant anyone else a meeting, sources tell me and @JeremyWoo . Both have believed in going No. 1 -- and now teams in the lottery are taking note of Peterson's decision to only give access to Washington.

item.title: Dybantsa and his camp hadn’t heard anything from the …
item.description: Up until a week ago, Dybantsa and his camp hadn’t heard anything from the Washington Wizards, who have the No. 1 pick, or the Utah Jazz at No. 2. Dybantsa has been very busy since the combine week in May. He was in Boston for his older sister’s graduation from Boston College, then Dybantsa and his father, Ace, flew to Paris for the French Open and continued training sessions with Zack Gonzales (the same trainer who went through the pre-draft process with Brooklyn Nets guard Egor Denim last year)

item.title: AJ Dybantsa visiting Wizards, Jazz next week?
item.description: Dybantsa is the projected No. 1 pick and will visit both the Wizards and Jazz next week. Typically, teams bring in their top option as close to the draft as possible to leave a lasting good impression and to prevent leaks from within the organization or on the player’s side. Dybantsa will only meet with the top two teams and will not travel to Memphis (third pick) or Chicago (fourth pick).

item.title: Alex Sarr undergoes surgery on fractured right foot
item.description: Shams Charania: Washington Wizards center Alex Sarr underwent surgery to repair a fractured right foot on Monday, sources tell ESPN. Sarr sustained the injury on a contact play during an offseason workout late last week. He is expected to be healthy for the start of the season.

item.title: Heat eyeing Trae Young as fallback if Giannis Antetokounmpo pursuit falls short?
item.description: I've been told by various sources that — in addition to the LA Clippers' Kawhi Leonard and Memphis' Ja Morant — Wizards point guard Trae Young has emerged as another "big fish" backup option for the Miami Heat in the event that Boston, Portland or some other team manages to outbid Pat Riley and Co. in the trade chase for Antetokounmpo.

item.title: Whispers are that the second sign is the authentic …
item.description: Darryn Peterson thinks he’s going No. 1, so much so that he visited only with the Wizards, blowing off the Jazz. What does that mean? Calling Dr. Freud, what does it mean? It could be a sign that Peterson doesn’t want to play in Utah. And it could be a sign that he’s absolutely convinced the Wiz will take him. No need to fiddle-faddle around with the Jazz. Whispers are that the second sign is the authentic one. Washington wants the smooth-shooting guard. But whispers the week before the draft of

item.title: There is an anticipation now, though, that external …
item.description: Young, by all accounts, relished the warm welcome he received from the Wizards and their fans and was initially projected to land an extension in the $40 million range annually from his new team. There is an anticipation now, though, that external interest from teams in need of an offensive (and specifically playmaking) boost could well surface and ignite Young's market depending on how things shake out with the draft and its corresponding trade activity.

item.title: "Darryn worked out with the Wizards and his camp came …
item.description: Ben Anderson: "Darryn worked out with the Wizards and his camp came away very confident he's going to be the number one overall pick, and therefore doesn't need to come to Utah because he's not going to be on the board."

item.title: The Jazz, according to league sources, weren’t …
item.description: The Jazz have had Dybantsa and Boozer for private workouts, according to league sources. Peterson canceled his workout this week amid reports that he believes he will be the No. 1 pick, and he hopes the Wizards select him. The Jazz, according to league sources, weren’t surprised by Peterson’s canceled workout. They anticipated it, on some levels. They also won’t hesitate to select Peterson if they determine the former Kansas star guard is the best player on the board when they pick.

item.title: Behind the scenes, there is some concern on Peterson’s …
item.description: The irony of Peterson’s camp revealing his preference for the Wizards is that Utah is the cleanest fit for him of the three teams at the top of the draft. The Jazz see him as a shooting guard who can play point guard. Behind the scenes, there is some concern on Peterson’s side that he and Jazz point guard Keyonte George share the same agency. But the Jazz firmly believe the two can play together.

item.title: Trae Young now drawing interest?
item.description: In his latest NBA Insider update for Bleacher Report, Jake Fischer revealed that the star guard is now generating a lot of interest out of the teams' desire to improve in the regular season and the high probability that tanking will fail out of the new NBA lottery system. "There is now some renewed interest in Trae Young, compared to where things were in February, where there’s not much interest," Fischer said.

item.title: "The overwhelming expectations still remains that Trae …
item.description: "The overwhelming expectations still remains that Trae Young is going to remain in Washington. I’ve been told that he really relished the warm welcome he received there post-trade deadline, Fischer said. " ... I do think he’s got a lot of enthusiasm for what the Wizards could do next season with the No. 1 pick, with Anthony Davis healthy, with Alex Sarr recovering well from offseason surgery. "We’ll see what actually comes to fruition in Washington, but I have been told to consider Trae Young as

item.title: Wizards viewed Trae Young as the most valuable free agent when trading for him
item.description: When the Washington Wizards acquired Trae Young for CJ McCollum and Corey Kispert, they effectively did their free agency shopping early, viewing Young as the most valuable free agent on the market, HoopsHype has learned. Young is excited for a fresh start with the Wizards and to begin the prime of his career in Washington. According to a league source who spoke with HoopsHype, Young hopes to remain a Wizard for a long time. While Young has a $48.97 million player option for the 2026-27 season,

item.title: Trae Young plans to decline player option for next season, become free agent
item.description: Marc J. Spears: Wizards star Trae Young plans to decline his $48.97 million player option for the 2026–27 season to become a FA on Monday, sources. Washington remains the front runner for the four-time NBA All-Star as he loves the team and DC, but he still expects multiple team max interest.

item.title: Wizards. AJ Dybantsa | 6-9 wing | 19 years old | BYU …
item.description: 1. Washington Wizards. AJ Dybantsa | 6-9 wing | 19 years old | BYU I’m sticking with Dybantsa here, but sources say that Washington continues due diligence and that the Wizards haven’t made a decision yet. One thing that has been made clear in recent days is that the team is doing a lot of research into Darryn Peterson to understand everything it needs to know about him. However, sources have also said not to read too much into the fact that Peterson is not currently scheduled to go to the Utah

item.title: Trae Young expected to re-sign with Wizards on three-year deal
item.description: Get Up: "He's going to re-sign with the Wizards, likely on a three-year, very large contract." @Windhorstespn gives an update on what could be next for Trae Young 🏀

item.title: Anthony Davis has communicated to Wizards a 'strong interest' in being part of what team is building
item.description: NBC 4 Sports: "He's communicated to me a strong interest in being part of what we're building" In a one-on-one with @JPFinlayNBCS , Monumental Basketball President Michael Winger discusses Anthony Davis and his future More of their interview this afternoon on News4

item.title: "Increased consideration" is the way one well-placed …
item.description: With only a few days to go before Tuesday night's first round of the NBA Draft, multiple draft experts have passed along that they legitimately believe Washington could select Kansas' Darryn Peterson over BYU's AJ Dybantsa with the No. 1 overall pick. "Increased consideration" is the way one well-placed insider put it.

item.title: Perkins adds the new GM role to his ESPN position and …
item.description: Shams Charania: Perkins adds the new GM role to his ESPN position and becomes the latest retired or active NBA player to take an administrative position in college basketball, joining most recently John Wall (HBCU Howard), Stephen Curry (Davidson), Trae Young (Oklahoma) and others.

item.title: D'Angelo Russell opts into 2026-27 player option to stay with Wizards
item.description: Jake Fischer: Per source: D’Angelo Russell has opted in to his 2026-27 player option with the Wizards worth $6 million.

item.title: I’m sticking with Dybantsa here, but sources say that …
item.description: I’m sticking with Dybantsa here, but sources say that Washington continues due diligence and that the Wizards haven’t made a decision yet. One thing that has been made clear in recent days is that the team is doing a lot of research into Darryn Peterson to understand everything it needs to know about him. However, sources have also said not to read too much into the fact that Peterson is not currently scheduled to go to the Utah Jazz to work out; they don’t think that means the Wizards have made

item.title: Wizards plan to draft AJ Dybantsa at No. 1?
item.description: The Washington Wizards currently plan to draft former BYU forward AJ Dybantsa with the No. 1 overall pick, sources told Dallas Hoops Journal, with the franchise centered on him as the 2026 NBA Draft opens Tuesday.

item.title: One potential target if Dallas were to trade down: …
item.description: One potential target if Dallas were to trade down: Washington big man Hannes Steinbach. Sources say that Steinbach, whose father Burkhard played alongside Mavericks legend Dirk Nowitzki on the German League team in their shared hometown of Wurzburg some 30 years ago, is among the prospects to have worked out for the Mavericks under consideration for the No. 9 pick.

item.title: “I’m just super versatile. 6’9 doing it all. Could …
item.description: AJ Dybantsa on what he brings to the table that other players don’t bring: “I’m just super versatile. 6’9 doing it all. Could play one through five. I did it this year. Small lineups. I was playing four and five. Bigger lineups. I’m bringing the ball up, throwing lobs. I’m an exciting player, so I fill seats. Want the fans, you know, to come see me play. And I’m just super relatable though. Like people just relate to me.” (Via @stephenasmith )

item.title: “I visited multiple teams because you never know what …
item.description: NBA Courtside: AJ Dybantsa says he wants and expects to be the number one draft pick: “I visited multiple teams because you never know what can happen on draft night, I was just being smart in my opinion. I want to get drafted number one though.” (Via @stephenasmith )

item.title: There have been rumbles in the last week that he might …
item.description: Marc Stein: "We've been reporting since March that Trae Young is going to stay with the Wizards, that it's probably going to be a three-year deal. I have not heard anything really to change my opinion there. But there have been rumbles in the last week that he might get some external interest, and look, his option deadline is actually Tuesday, the day of the draft is when he officially has to make that decision."

item.title: AJ Dybantsa still the favorite for No. 1 draft pick?
item.description: Rival executives continue to speculate that the Washington Wizards will take AJ Dybantsa. And if they do surprise the league, it will be with Darryn Peterson, though sources say Washington has remained very tight-lipped during the process.

item.title: Not much has changed for Cameron Boozer as he enters …
item.description: Not much has changed for Cameron Boozer as he enters what should be a straightforward draft night. Sources say he visited Memphis last week and previously visited Chicago and Utah, covering all bases other than Washington. Although he is under consideration in Utah, where his father is part of the front office, rival teams believe the Jazz will have a hard time passing on Dybantsa or Peterson.

item.title: Darryn Peterson on only working out for the Washington …
item.description: Michael Scotto: Darryn Peterson on only working out for the Washington Wizards with the No. 1 pick: “Just a decision that was made. I’m definitely not dodging any team. Whoever takes me, I’m going to come in and be delighted to be there. Wherever that happens to be, they’ll get the best me.”

item.title: Trae Young agrees to a four-year contract with Washington
item.description: Shams Charania: BREAKING: Trae Young intends to sign a four-year, approximately $212 million deal to stay with the Washington Wizards, with a player option in Year 4, sources tell ESPN. The four-time NBA All-Star declined his $49M player option for a long-term commitment ahead of free agency.

item.title: The Wizards and Young’s representatives, CAA’s Co-Head …
item.description: Shams Charania: The Wizards and Young’s representatives, CAA’s Co-Head of Basketball Aaron Mintz, agents Ray Young, Andrew Morrison and Co-Head of Basketball Austin Brown, land on a deal that will be less than the maximum for Young with Washington, but equal to the max he could have signed elsewhere. Young – who wanted a trade to one team only, the Wizards, in January – stamps his desire to be in D.C. and elevate the team’s rising young core.

item.title: Trae Young jumps into Top-20 all-time in guaranteed NBA earnings
item.description: Jorge Sierra: With his new contract, Trae Young is now Top 20 all-time in guaranteed NBA earnings.

item.title: Wizards not engaging in trade talks for No. 1 draft pick
item.description: Marc J. Spears: The Washington Wizards are expected to keep the No. 1 pick and aren’t engaging in trade opportunities, a source told @andscape.

item.title: AJ Dybantsa: Utah and Washington can automatically be a playoff team if they add me
item.description: AJ Dybantsa: "I think both teams, Utah and Washington, they have a deep enough roster that if they add me, that we can automatically be a playoff team. And that rookie year, obviously."

item.title: Wizards front office expected Trae Young to command max salary offers
item.description: The NBA rumor mill, which is often inaccurate, predicted months ago that any new deal between Trae Young and the Wizards would average no more than $40 million annually. But in recent weeks, Wizards decision-makers became convinced that, with the NBA’s new anti-tanking measures compelling more teams to compete, Young was going to command maximum-salary contract offers from other franchises through either a straight free-agent signing or a sign-and-trade proposal.

item.title: For Washington, the difference between signing Young …
item.description: Because the Wizards hold Young’s Bird rights, the Wizards had the latitude to offer Young up to a five-year contract with 8 percent annual raises. But Young’s new four-year deal instead features 5 percent year-over-year raises, the maximum year-over-year raise that any other team could have offered Young as a non-Bird free agent. For Washington, the difference between signing Young to 5 percent raises instead of 8 percent raises will amount to a total savings of $8.9 million over four years.

item.title: Adjusting these figures slightly, as Young's new …
item.description: Keith Smith: Adjusting these figures slightly, as Young's new contract is reported to start at the 30% max amount of $49.5M: 26-27: $49.5M 27-28: $51.9M 28-29: $54.5M 29-30: $56.9M (player option) Total: $212.85M (matches what Young could have gotten from another team as a FA)

item.title: This doesn't preclude them, they say, from taking …
item.description: Brian Windhorst: "Speaking to the Wizards today, they are very happy to get Trae Young under contract. This doesn't preclude them, they say, from taking Peterson with the number one pick. Now, whether we believe that or not is a different story."

item.title: Anthony Davis and his camp have been seeking a max …
item.description: Fullcourtpass: Anthony Davis and his camp have been seeking a max extension, and it’s plausible to consider him a potential trade candidate out of Washington, per @JakeLFischer (h/t @TheDunkCentral)

item.title: Blazers will be looking to trade for Anthony Davis is he becomes available
item.description: In his latest NBA insider report for Bleacher Report, Jake Fischer announced that the Blazers are open to the possibility of trading for the star big man should the Washington Wizards like to entertain a trade. "Portland, to our understanding, as they have been floating around and poking around in Giannis trade conversations, we've been told that they would look to pivot to Anthony Davis on the trade market," Fischer said. "They obviously have Jaylen Brown interest as well. "We've been told that

item.title: Originally signed a NIL shoe deal with Nike in early …
item.description: Nick DePaula: AJ Dybantsa is the #1 pick. AJ originally signed a NIL shoe deal with Nike in early 2024, and is now on a new multi-year extension as he begins his NBA career. Deal will include featuring AJ across upcoming GT models and already includes his own Player Exclusive logo.

item.title: Wizards select AJ Dybantsa at No. 1
item.description: Michael Scotto: Sources: The Washington Wizards are selecting AJ Dybantsa with the No. 1 pick in the 2026 NBA Draft.

item.title: AJ Dybantsa will spearhead the Wizards youth movement …
item.description: Cyro Asseo: AJ Dybantsa will spearhead the Wizards youth movement alongside Kyshawn George, Alex Sarr and the duo of Trae Young-Anthony Davis. His ability to get to the rim and draw fouls will be huge for this young team.

item.title: "It just means a lot. ... Obviously I have a lot more …
item.description: NBA on ESPN: "It just means a lot. ... Obviously I have a lot more work to do, but a testament to all my hard work and all the discipline and sacrifices that I make." AJ Dybantsa sits down with @saltersl after being drafted No. 1 to the Wizards 🏀

item.title: Ace Dybantsa tells @andscape his son, 2026 No. 1 NBA …
item.description: Marc J. Spears: Ace Dybantsa tells @andscape his son, 2026 No. 1 NBA Draft pick AJ Dybtansa of the Washington Wizards, is wearing pins on his draft suit to represent late former Kentucky star Terrence Clark, his mother’s native Jamaica and his father’s native the Republic of the Congo.

item.title: I’m happy that we’re going to the District. We’re …
item.description: Marc J. Spears: “I feel great happy. I’m happy that we’re going to the District. We’re coming!,” Ace Dybantsa said.

item.title: The story has been written🙏🏽 The City is Ready!
item.description: Trae Young: Welcome to DC @AJ_Dybantsa !! The story has been written🙏🏽 The City is Ready! We’ll talk about that # soon 😉

item.title: "I found out when y'all found out. I didn't get a …
item.description: SportsCenter: "I found out when y'all found out. I didn't get a call, my dad had my phone the whole time." AJ Dybantsa on the moment he was selected No. 1 overall by the Wizards 🙌

item.title: I'm told he did get a pretty hard workout in when he …
item.description: Oh No He Didn’t: Shams: "When AJ Dybantsa visited the Wizards earlier this month in DC I know AJ Dybantsa said he didn't work out for anyone. I'm told he did get a pretty hard workout in when he was on that visit, had dinner with them, hung out with all of them"

item.title: AJ Dybantsa on what his dad means to him: "So many …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: AJ Dybantsa on what his dad means to him: "So many sacrifices that he's made. He quit his job just to retire to come move in with me at Utah Prep so I'd be safe. Sending me over to California by myself to go get a better basketball experience. All the little stuff that I didn't notice he was doing at a young age obviously it's paying off now. But he's my dad. I mean, he acts as my manager. He does a whole bunch of stuff that he doesn't have to do. But I just give him the utmost

item.title: AJ Dybantsa: 'I’ve been dreaming about this since I was about 12 years old'
item.description: Dybantsa had imagined that exact moment, becoming the first pick in the draft, for years. So, how did the moment compare to what he had envisioned in his dreams? “The reality is way better,” Dybantsa told The Athletic, a Wizards cap still atop his head. “I’ve been dreaming about this since I was about 12 years old. And seven years later, I’m accomplishing my dream, and it just feels super surreal, and I’m just happy I got to spend it with my family.”

item.title: AJ asked league officials to use his given name when …
item.description: His joy shone through in the seconds after Silver spoke. He hugged his mom, Chelsea; his godparents; his older sister, Samarra; his younger sister, Jasmyn; and, finally, his dad, Anicet Sr., nicknamed Ace. AJ asked league officials to use his given name when he was drafted as a way of honoring his father. On his custom-made Kenzo suit, near his left lapel, he wore a pin of the flag of the Republic of the Congo, where his dad is from, and a pin of the flag of Jamaica, where his mom is from. He wo

item.title: Magic trade No. 46 pick Felix Okpara to Wizards
item.description: Michael Scotto: TRADE: The Washington Wizards are acquiring Felix Okpara, the No. 46 pick in the 2026 NBA Draft, from the Orlando Magic in exchange for the No. 51 and 60 picks, per league sources.

item.title: Orlando traded No. 46 Felix Okpara to Washington for …
item.description: Shams Charania: Sources: Orlando traded No. 46 Felix Okpara to Washington for Nos. 51 and 60.

item.title: And with AD, he wants to be here, we want him here. …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Will Dawkins: "Can't comment on the Trae one just yet but we feel pretty good where that's headed. And with AD, he wants to be here, we want him here. We'll have that conversation in the middle of August when we can officially have that"

item.title: “He’s one of the rare athletes, rare humans with a …
item.description: NBA on ESPN: “He’s one of the rare athletes, rare humans with a combination to be a very special player in the future.” Wizards GM Will Dawkins on the decision to draft AJ Dybantsa with the No. 1 pick 🙌

item.title: Orlando used the No. 51 pick to select Nelson, the …
item.description: After remaining out of the first round of the NBA draft on Tuesday night, the Magic made multiple trades throughout the second round on Wednesday before ultimately landing on South Florida forward Izaiyah Nelson. Entering the night with only the No. 46 pick, Orlando sent that pick, which was used to select Tennessee forward Felix Okpara, to the Washington Wizards in exchange for the Nos. 51 and 60 draft picks, a league source confirmed to the Orlando Sentinel. Shortly after, the Magic then sent

item.title: AJ Dybantsa on taking Trae Young's #3 jersey number: …
item.description: [Finlay] AJ Dybantsa on taking Trae Young's #3 jersey number: "I was trolling about but... if he wants to give me the number I'll take the number"

item.title: Nike offered AJ Dybantsa his first sneaker deal at age …
item.description: Nike offered AJ Dybantsa his first sneaker deal at age 16, and he said yes (even though his friend Shaquille O’Neal wanted him at Reebok) because “Nike’s the best marketing sports brand out there. So when they offered me, that was like a no brainer. Being in the same conversation as LeBron, Kobe, Kevin Durant, Devin Booker. Like I said, kind of a no-brainer.” Nike wanted to know all about him. He told them the taxi driver story, told them he was Starboy, told him he already had his own logo, too

item.title: What do you like to do for fun? AJ Dybantsa: Sleep.
item.description: What do you like to do for fun? AJ Dybantsa: Sleep. I've been golfing and fishing a couple times, but I've been kind of busy. I kind of didn't really have time to do it this year.

item.title: KeShawn Murphy has agreed to an Exhibit-10 contract …
item.description: Jon Chepkevich: NEWS: Auburn’s KeShawn Murphy has agreed to an Exhibit-10 contract with the Washington Wizards, sources tell DraftExpress. 10.7 points, 6.8 rebounds, 1.3 assists, and 2 stocks per game for the Tigers this season. Brings defensive versatility and can play out of the short roll.

item.title: The Washington Wizards are hiring Tevon Saddler as …
item.description: Michael Scotto: Source: The Washington Wizards are hiring Tevon Saddler as head coach of their G League affiliate, the Capital City Go-Go. The Aberdeen, Maryland native joins his hometown team as the second-youngest G League coach at 31 years old. He has a strong background in player development

item.title: AJ Dybantsa on John Wall's advice: "Really tap into …
item.description: Chase Hughes: AJ Dybantsa on John Wall's advice: "Really tap into the community, they love you here.... I saw the video of [fans] at the Anthem."

item.title: "I fill seats," said he started to notice the crowds …
item.description: Chase Hughes: AJ Dybantsa on his famous line "I fill seats," said he started to notice the crowds change when he was in the 8th grade & he showed up to his HS and they had yellow tape on the sidelines to help contain the fans. Then he played at TD Garden with BYU and saw the seats all packed.

item.title: "He got to his middy and I take huge pride getting to …
item.description: Chase Hughes: AJ Dybantsa on his viral video comparing himself to Len Bias: "He got to his middy and I take huge pride getting to my middy... I started watching his film probably a couple months ago. I started pulling up his film on TikTok and I didn't know who he was."

item.title: Official: @AJ_Dybantsa will wear No. 4 🔥
item.description: Washington Wizards: Official: @AJ_Dybantsa will wear No. 4 🔥

item.title: AJ Dybantsa: 'I am definitely grateful that the Wizards picked up Trae and picked up AD'
item.description: AJ Dybantsa: "I am definitely grateful that the Wizards picked up Trae and picked up AD. I am going to have vets, I am going to have a guy that wants a championship, I am going to have a guy that is a multiple-time All-Star that I can ask questions to."

item.title: AJ Dybantsa: "I seen him on TikTok, there was a dude …
item.description: AJ Dybantsa: "I seen him on TikTok, there was a dude that put me better than Michael Jordan. I believe Michael Jordan is the GOAT, so I decided to watch the video and see he was 6'6, 220 with a 40-inch vertical, and I was like that kind of sounds like me. So I decided to watch some film, I seen him get into his mid-range a lot and I take pride in my mid-range, so I kind of seen the comparison a little bit."

item.title: ESPN and ABC's broadcast of the 2026 NBA Draft first …
item.description: ESPN and ABC's broadcast of the 2026 NBA Draft first round averaged 4.2 million viewers Tuesday night, a 10% increase from the prior year, the network announced. The audience peaked at 5.5 million viewers during the 8:15 p.m. ET quarter hour. AJ Dybantsa went first overall to the Washington Wizards, followed by Darryn Peterson to the Utah Jazz at No. 2, Cameron Boozer to the Memphis Grizzlies at No. 3, Caleb Wilson to the Chicago Bulls at No. 4 and Keaton Wagler to the Los Angeles Clippers at No

item.title: The Blazers could also pursue a trade for Anthony …
item.description: Portland previously attempted to trade for Giannis Antetokounmpo this offseason, but he communicated that he didn't have interest in signing long-term with the franchise. The Blazers could also pursue a trade for Anthony Davis this offseason as the Washington Wizards consider their options. The Blazers could offer a combination of draft capital, Jerami Grant's expiring contract as salary ballast, and young players such as Scoot Henderson and Sharron Sharpe.

item.title: Seth Trimble is joining the Wizards after going …
item.description: Former UNC guard Seth Trimble is joining the Wizards after going undrafted, according to an announcement from the Tar Heels’ men’s basketball team (Twitter link).

item.title: “When it comes to prospects I’ve evaluated in my 10 …
item.description: Most often, AJ himself speaks in short, direct sentences, and if he doesn’t know you, it’s unlikely that you’ll receive a smile. But on the court he emanates confidence, barking instructions and encouragement to his teammates while shredding defenses. In the final minute of the first quarter, he drills a 3-pointer at one end before swatting one away at the other, sending a jolt of electricity through the arena. “When it comes to prospects I’ve evaluated in my 10 years, AJ is easily one of the be

item.title: “He’d come home crying because the bigger kids were …
item.description: AJ showed little interest in sports as a toddler but loved superheroes. When AJ was 5, his father bought a Spider-Man mini hoop, attached it to his door, and taught him to take shots from his bed. AJ was rapt. Ace later installed a 25-by-25-foot blacktop court in the backyard, and whenever someone was looking for AJ, that was the place to start. He carried his basketball everywhere and slept with it tucked under his arm. In sixth grade, AJ started working with trainer Brandon Ball, whose session

item.title: Warriors trying to trade for Anthony Davis to entice LeBron James to join
item.description: The Warriors are attempting to trade for Wizards big Anthony Davis and then sign Lakers free agent forward LeBron James, according to multiple league sources.

item.title: Sources say the Warriors hope adding Davis would help …
item.description: Sources say the Warriors hope adding Davis would help lure LeBron from Los Angeles to the Bay Area once free agency officially opens on June 30. The pitch would be simple: reunite with AD, team up with Steph Curry and Draymond Green, play for Steve Kerr, and chase one more championship with a roster of legends.

item.title: Portland Trail Blazers are adding Mike Williams as an …
item.description: Michael Scotto: Sources: The Portland Trail Blazers are adding Mike Williams as an assistant coach. Williams moves to the front of the bench in Portland on coach Micah Nori’s staff. He’s been an assistant coach with the Jazz, a G League head coach, and Wizards player development assistant coach.

item.title: “The notion of them trying to trade for AD first as a …
item.description: To sweeten the deal, journalist Kevin O’Connor reported that Davis could also be lured to Dub Nation. However, NBA insider Marc Stein was quick to pump the brakes on those reports, explaining why such a blockbuster move is far from materializing. “Based on everything I was told tonight, I don’t think there is anything active there,” Marc Stein stated on The All-NBA Podcast. “The notion of them trying to trade for AD first as a lure for LeBron, hard to see that happening in that sequence… I think

item.title: Draymond Green's opt-out gives Warriors room to pursue Anthony Davis and recruit LeBron James
item.description: Shams Charania: The opt-out comes days after the Warriors said they expect Green to pick up the $27.7M option. Instead Green delivers Golden State time and space to pursue Davis with the Wizards and convince James to join Stephen Curry, Green and his old Lakers championship teammate in the Bay.

item.title: Draymond Green declines player option
item.description: Shams Charania: Golden State Warriors forward Draymond Green is declining his $27.7 million player option to become a free agent, sources tell ESPN. This move gives the Warriors flexibility to pursue LeBron James in free agency and Anthony Davis via trade to form a Big 4.

item.title: The Warriors have A LOT more flexibility now for …
item.description: Kevin O'Connor: This is a huge development. The Warriors have A LOT more flexibility now for pursuing LeBron James AND Anthony Davis, which as I reported last night is their plan.

item.title: AJ Dybantsa: "The Wizards haven't been to the playoffs …
item.description: AJ Dybantsa: "The Wizards haven't been to the playoffs in some time, so I just want to make the playoffs year one. I think we have the team to do it, getting Anthony Davis, getting Trae Young and having our young core. But ultimate goal is I'll start getting D-Wade, I always wanted to be a Hall of Famer, but he says the goal I should have is to be a top 100 player. So I think 20 years from now, I answer the league 20 years from now, it will be the 100-year anniversary of the NBA. And you know, l

item.title: Rich Paul met with Wizards to discuss Anthony Davis' future with the franchise
item.description: Anthony Davis' agent, Rich Paul, met with the Washington Wizards on Sunday to discuss his future with the franchise. Davis is eligible this offseason for a four-year extension worth up to $275 million this offseason with the Wizards. As the Wizards recently signed Trae Young to a four-year, $212 million contract, Davis is clearly gauging whether he could receive a similar commitment from the franchise. Davis has also been the subject of trade rumors involving the Golden State Warriors with a pot

item.title: "I'm sure that Anthony Davis could be given a contract …
item.description: "Anthony Davis just saw Trae Young get a $200 million new contract with the Wizards," said ESPN's Brian Windhorst on Monday's episode of Get Up. "Yesterday, Rich Paul, who represents Draymond Green and LeBron and Anthony Davis met with the Wizards to discuss the future. "I'm sure that Anthony Davis could be given a contract that would make him very happy to be in Washington. Maybe the Wizards are prepared to do that. The Wizards want to win immediately. "If not, the Warriors could potentially co

item.title: They would presumably swap Jimmy Butler and picks for …
item.description: Yossi Gozlan: Warriors cap situation after extending Kristaps Porzingis and De'Anthony Melton presumably opting out. The LeBron James-Anthony Davis pipedream is still alive. They would presumably swap Jimmy Butler and picks for Davis. Then they could fit James with $15 million non-taxpayer mid-level under the first apron hard cap IF Draymond Green takes the same contract as Porzingis, and IF they also find a taker for Moses Moody.

item.title: No tangible movement for Anthony Davis to Golden State talks
item.description: That could also mean exploring a trade for Washington center Anthony Davis to help lure James, though there has yet to be tangible movement on that front, league sources said.

item.title: It is my understanding that AD's representatives and …
item.description: Brian Windhorst: "Anthony Davis has the same agent, Rich Paul. It is my understanding that AD's representatives and the Wizards met yesterday about a possible contract extension. He can't sign an extension until August, but this is when you would sort of talk about that. AD has one year left on his contract plus a player option, but one guaranteed year.

item.title: "It is possible that the Warriors could call the …
item.description: Brian Windhorst: "It is possible that the Warriors could call the Wizards and make an offer and say we would love to trade for Anthony Davis, and that Anthony Davis could be traded and LeBron could come in free agency and those things could happen. Yes, it is possible... and then Draymond could then resign for a lower number and then add years onto the back to make himself whole. All of those things are possible. I will also tell you that the Warriors routinely, every transaction window, and wha

item.title: The Wizards are over here saying like, what about us? …
item.description: Bobby Marks: "I think what Mike (Dunleavy) and the group don't want to do is trade for Anthony Davis and give up a ton and then don't have LeBron coming. So like, that would be probably the worst-case scenario to think, right? Because it's going to, because here's the thing, and I said this on TV today, it's like, everybody's making it, we're talking about this and it's great. And the Wizards are over here saying like, what about us? You know, like, what is the incentive for the Wizards to trade

item.title: Wizards interested in bringing back Russell Westbrook?
item.description: League sources tell The Stein Line that Washington has a level interest in Russell Westbrook. The nine-time former All-Star's final season of the four in his career in which he averaged a triple-double (22.2 PPG, 11.5 RPG and 11.7 APG) came in 2020-21 with the Wizards.

item.title: League sources say that the Warriors certainly haven't …
item.description: League sources say that the Warriors certainly haven't been operating under the premise that they must adhere to any mandate that James and Davis are somehow a package deal. If James becomes truly available once free agency actually begins and receptive to such pitches, Golden State will be trying to convince him to sign for an estimated $15 million. And Green, to be clear, is going nowhere. He's expected to sign a new multi-year deal after the Warriors sort out the rest of their roster business

item.title: This is going end up with Draymond getting a new …
item.description: [Windhorst] "The people on the league I'm talking to today are giving me the side eye and they're going, this is going end up with Draymond getting a new contract in GS, LeBron getting a new contract in LA and AD getting a new contract in Washington."

item.title: Yaxel said his friends spent all day texting him and …
item.description: Nick Friedell: Yaxel said his friends spent all day texting him and asking how he would fit in playing with Steph, Draymond, LeBron and AD.

item.title: The Washington Wizards decline their team option for …
item.description: Keith Smith: The Washington Wizards decline their team option for Jamir Watkins, a league source told @spotrac . The Wizards did tender a qualifying offer to Watkins. He'll now be a restricted free agent this offseason.

item.title: Anthony Davis to Warriors 'unlikely'
item.description: Brian Windhorst: The Warriors still have the ability to re-sign Draymond Green to a reasonable contract number and to try to add LeBron James, but it's really, really narrow and they would really need to make some other moves to build up their team. Right now, I'm taking the Anthony Davis thing off the front burner. I do not have data that says that that's likely to happen.

item.title: The actions were that Draymond Green opted out of …
item.description: Brian Windhorst: I like actions over words. The actions were that Draymond Green opted out of his contract. That gives the Warriors flexibility, but I don't think the Warriors think they're getting Anthony Davis. Do you want to know why I think they will? Because they signed Kristaps Porzingis. And this contract and Porzingis did not play a lot of games last year for anybody, much less for the Warriors. But you've got to have big men to compete in the Western Conference. They had to handle this

item.title: Warriors 'at the front of the line' in pursuit of LeBron James
item.description: Marc Stein: The Warriors are at the front of the line in pursuit of LeBron James in free agency and league sources tell @TheSteinLine that Golden State is not operating as though it has a mandate to also pursue an Anthony Davis trade.

item.title: Wizards shut down trade talks for Anthony Davis
item.description: Marc J. Spears: The Washington Wizards are not interested in trading 10-time NBA All-Star forward Anthony Davis and are not listening to trade offers for him, a source told @espn @andscape. Another NBA team exec says Wizards GM Will Dawkins is a huge fan of AD.

item.title: The Warriors are now signaling that Davis isn't in …
item.description: The Warriors are now signaling that Davis isn't in their plans, according to league sources who spoke on condition of anonymity to describe ongoing deliberations. Conversely, the Washington Wizards, the club that employs Davis, is very against the prospect of trading him to the Warriors (or, for now, anywhere), those sources said.

item.title: All of that, according to multiple NBA sources, is …
item.description: Is LeBron’s interest contingent on the Warriors acquiring his old Lakers teammate and fellow Paul client Anthony Davis, which would take the Warriors putting Jimmy Butler into any offer with the Wizards for salary-matching purposes? And would the Warriors have to commit to a long-term extension with Davis as part of this mammoth undertaking? All of that, according to multiple NBA sources, is mostly being assumed but has not been explicitly demanded by LeBron or Paul. And the Davis/Butler part of

item.title: As I’ve said for months, I think Davis will be on the …
item.description: Are people in the Wizards organization laughing at these Anthony Davis to the Warriors rumors? — @Faazzla The Wizards have no interest in trading Anthony Davis. As I’ve said for months, I think Davis will be on the Wizards’ roster to begin the regular season.

item.title: League sources told Sharania and ESPN's Anthony Slater …
item.description: League sources told Sharania and ESPN's Anthony Slater that a Warriors pursuit of Davis could be used as a motivational tactic to lure James to Golden State. The two won a championship together in 2020 with the Lakers and have remained close friends. However, there has yet to be tangible movement on the Davis front, league sources said.

item.title: Shelburne: Warriors want commitment from LeBron James before pursuing Anthony Davis
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Ramona on Lebron possibly going to the Warriors: "From what I understand this is a sequencing issue. They would want a commitment from Lebron James before they would go make a move for somebody like Anthony Davis. As I understand it now there haven't been any substantive conversations between the Warriors and the Washington Wizards about Anthony Davis...yet"

item.title: Anthony Davis: 🤣🤣🤣🤣🤣 Twit
item.description: Anthony Davis: 🤣🤣🤣🤣🤣 Twitter User: Anthony Davis practically begged Rob Pelinka to get him a CENTER alongside him for YEARS… Rob didn’t budge AD got back doored.. LeBron James lowkey got back doored.. and NOW he wanna be a competent GM now that the duo that won you a championship is gone.. you can’t make this s*** up LOL 🐍

item.title: Jamir Watkins returns to Washington on a two-way deal
item.description: Michael Scotto: The Washington Wizards have signed Jamir Watkins to a two-way contract.

item.title: Wizards waive Leaky Black
item.description: Michael Scotto: The Washington Wizards have waived two-way forward Leaky Black.

item.title: Kyshawn George says the Washington Wizards are …
item.description: Libaan Osman: Kyshawn George says the Washington Wizards are starting to look “scary” after adding No. 1 pick AJ Dybantsa. George also on Canadian teammate Will Riley: “Super skillful, wants to work, he’s in the gym everyday with me.”

item.title: The $17M salary of Aldama goes into the $20.8M …
item.description: Bobby Marks: The $17M salary of Aldama goes into the $20.8M exception created from the Anthony Davis trade in February. Aldama has 2 years left: $17M and $17M (T) Johnson has 2 years left: $3.2M and $5.4M (T)

item.title: Lakers interested in Andre Drummond, Jonas Valanciunas, Kevon Looney
item.description: Shams Charania: The Lakers will now focus on finding Walker Kessler's backup on the market -- with centers such as Andre Drummond, Jonas Valanciunas and Kevon Looney among those being considered, sources said. The Wizards took a patient approach to the start of free agency and now land a durable, reliable center to join Anthony Davis and Alex Sarr in the frontcourt.

item.title: Lakers trade Deandre Ayton to Wizards for Jaden Hardy, draft capital
item.description: Shams Charania: Just in: The Los Angeles Lakers are trading Deandre Ayton to the Washington Wizards for Jaden Hardy and two Wizards second-round picks in 2031 and 2032, sources tell ESPN.

item.title: The Lakers are now pursuing both a wing defender and a …
item.description: Marc Stein: The Lakers are now pursuing both a wing defender and a backup big man after lining up a Deandre Ayton-to- Washington trade which adds $2 million in financial wiggle room for their summer business, league sources say. More NBA from @JakeLFischer and me: tinyurl.com/3bk3v449

item.title: Jazz front office and Keyonte's agent finalized an …
item.description: Sarah Todd: Jazz front office and Keyonte's agent finalized an agreement for him to be an assistant coach for a single summer league game (July 9 vs. Wizards). The agreement came together after Keyonte drew up a game-changing play in practice.

item.title: Lakers interested in Kevon Looney, Jonas Valanciunas
item.description: Looney remains on the Lakers' radar, sources say, as does Nuggets center Jonas Valančiūnas. The latter, remember, was a Lakers free agent target two summers ago before Valančiūnas opted to sign a multiyear deal with Washington instead. Sources say that the Lakers' interest, furthermore, is certainly welcomed by the Nuggets, who have made Valančiūnas available in trade conversations since Denver's season ended and especially in connection with the recent draft as the Nuggets explored variou

item.title: Patrick Ewing joins Wizards as assistant coach
item.description: Shams Charania: Georgetown and New York Knicks legend Patrick Ewing has agreed to become an assistant coach for the Washington Wizards and head coach Brian Keefe, sources tell ESPN. Ewing has been in an Ambassador role with the Knicks and now returns on the coaching sidelines in D.C.

item.title: Patrick Ewing in talks to join Wizards' coaching staff
item.description: Ian Begley: SNY sources: Hall of Famer and Knick great Patrick Ewing is in talks with the Wizards to join the club’s coaching staff. Ewing has been an advisor and Basketball Ambassador with the Knicks for the past two seasons, was with the team throughout its 2026 title run.

item.title: Steve Clifford joining Wizards as coaching advisor
item.description: Shams Charania: Longtime NBA coach Steve Clifford is also joining the Wizards as a coaching advisor, sources tell ESPN. Clifford, who has held similar advisor roles for the Suns and Nets in recent years, coached as an assistant with Ewing in Orlando (2007-12). Ewing then served as an assistant under Clifford in Charlotte from 2013-17.

item.title: Steve Clifford joining Wizards
item.description: Shams Charania: Longtime NBA coach Steve Clifford is also joining the Wizards as a coaching advisor, sources tell ESPN. Clifford, who has held similar advisor roles for the Suns and Nets in recent years, coached as an assistant with Ewing in Orlando (2007-12). Ewing then served as an assistant under Clifford in Charlotte from 2013-17.sources tell ESPN. Clifford, who has held similar advisor roles for the Suns and Nets in recent years, coached as an assistant with Ewing in Orlando (2007-12). Ewin

item.title: David Vanterpool moving to Wizards' front office
item.description: Joshua Robbins: David Vanterpool, who has worked as the Wizards' lead assistant coach under Brian Keefe, is moving into a front-office role with the team, a team source told @TheAthleticNBA . Adam Caporn, who already is on staff, will be the Wizards' lead assistant coach.

item.title: The Washington Wizards announced today the following …
item.description: The Washington Wizards announced today the following coaching staff updates: · Adam Caporn, Wizards lead assistant · Patrick Ewing, Wizards assistant coach · Cody Toppert, Wizards assistant coach · Steve Clifford, Wizards coaching advisor · Tevon Saddler, Capital City Go-Go head coach/Wizards assistant coach · T.J. Sorrentine, Las Vegas Summer League head coach

item.title: David Vanterpool transitions to a basketball …
item.description: David Vanterpool transitions to a basketball operations role in the front office after serving as a Wizards assistant coach the past three seasons.

item.title: Trae Young's new contract has a trade kicker included
item.description: Michael Scotto: Trae Young has a 7.5% trade kicker in his four-year, $212 million deal with the Washington Wizards, league sources told @hoopshype . The agreement and Young having a fourth-year player option was first reported by ESPN.

item.title: Wizards have released their 2026 Summer League roster
item.description: Greg Finberg: The Wizards have released their 2026 Summer League roster

item.title: The Washington Wizards and Felix Okpara have agreed to …
item.description: Michael Scotto: The Washington Wizards and Felix Okpara have agreed to a two-way contract, league sources told @hoopshype . Okpara was the 46th pick in the 2026 NBA Draft and was an SEC All-Defensive Team selection. He was acquired by the Wizards from the Orlando Magic in a trade on draft night.

item.title: Warriors believe they aren’t at the top of LeBron’s …
item.description: NBA Courtside: The Warriors believe they aren’t at the top of LeBron’s list unless they can package Anthony Davis, per @ShamsCharania

item.title: Hardy will wear No. 7 for LAL
