item.description: Dave McMenamin: The Lakers make the Deandre Ayton-Jaden Hardy trade with WAS official. Hardy will wear No. 7 for LAL.

item.title: Khris Middleton agrees to sign-and-trade deal to return to Wizards
item.description: Shams Charania: Just in: Free agent Khris Middleton has agreed on a three-year, $17.6 million deal with the Washington Wizards via sign-and-trade, agent Mike Lindeman of Excel Sports Management tells ESPN. The 14-year veteran returns to D.C. where he spent parts of last two seasons as a leader with championship presence.

item.title: Mavericks will create a $5.6 million trade exception …
item.description: Yossi Gozlan: The Mavericks will create a $5.6 million trade exception by sign and trading Khris Middleton to these terms. The Wizards can absorb him into their $13.4 million trade exception which expires tomorrow.

item.title: Dallas is sending Middleton to the Wizards via …
item.description: Shams Charania: Dallas is sending Middleton to the Wizards via sign-and-trade as part of a larger six-team deal that includes, in total, the Mavericks, Wizards, Clippers, Pistons, Bucks and Grizzlies. It involves previously agreed upon deals such as John Collins, Gary Harris and Taurean Prince (Detroit), Isaiah Stewart (Memphis), Santi Aldama (Mavericks), Caris LeVert (Bucks), etc.

item.title: The Wizards have the $13.4M Kelly Olynyk trade …
item.description: Bobby Marks: Likely trade structure The Wizards have the $13.4M Kelly Olynyk trade exception that is set to expire on July 9. Deandrea Ayton- $8.1M Khris Middleton- $5.3M

item.title: D'Angelo Russell heads to Memphis
item.description: Shams Charania: As part of the elaborate six-team trade, the Washington Wizards are sending D'Angelo Russell, a future second-round pick and a future second-round pick swap to the Memphis Grizzlies, sources tell ESPN.

item.title: Because of the Khris Middleton sign-and-trade, Dallas …
item.description: Bobby Marks: A nice job by the Mavericks front office on this deal. Because of the Khris Middleton sign-and-trade, Dallas will preserve the big $20.8M Anthony Davis trade exception. To break it down on a smaller scale: Middleton/AJ Johnson for Santi Aldama

item.title: One thing I learned about Khris Middleton from people …
item.description: Grant Afseth: One thing I learned about Khris Middleton from people with the Washington Wizards is just how valuable he was as a mentor. He proved to be a helpful presence for Cooper Flagg during his time in Dallas. It's easy to see why the Wizards wanted him back.

item.title: Khris Middleton's contract has just one fully guaranteed year
item.description: Josh Robbins: The second season on Khris Middleton's new three-year sign-and-trade deal with the Wizards will be partially guaranteed, while the third season will be non-guaranteed, per source.

item.title: By helping Khris Middleton get to Washington via …
item.description: Marc Stein: By helping Khris Middleton get to Washington via sign-and-trade, Dallas can acquire Santi Aldama and Marcus Sasser without touching its $20.3M TPE from the Anthony Davis trade or its $15M MLE. The Mavs can then formally sign Tarik Biberović after the six-team trade is official.

item.title: Warriors believe they are not on LeBron James' list unless they can trade for Anthony Davis
item.description: "I think LeBron's been open-minded, but the Warriors' whole plan going into free agency was, 'Okay, let's see if we can get Stephen Curry, Draymond Green, LeBron James, and Anthony Davis,'" said ESPN's Shams Charania on Stephen A. Smith's radio show. "I think the big draw for LeBron with the Warriors would be seeing if there's an avenue to be a package deal with Davis," added Charania. "Short of that, I don't think the Warriors are high on his list, and I think the Warriors themselves believe th

item.title: Despite some league sources believing that an …
item.description: Part of LeBron’s appeal to the Warriors is the low risk. It would be a short-term signing without the loss of a draft pick to acquire him. Despite some league sources believing that an asset-dependent trade for Anthony Davis might be necessary to lure James, the Warriors haven’t been told that directly and haven’t been engaging Washington on it, team sources said.

item.title: “No matter where you send me I’m still dangerous”
item.description: Fullcourtpass: D’Angelo Russell on the Wizards trading him to the Grizzlies “No matter where you send me I’m still dangerous”

item.title: Stein‬: I'm told that six-team trade involving the …
item.description: ‪Marc Stein‬: I'm told that six-team trade involving the Pistons, Clippers, Grizzlies, Bucks, Wizards and Mavericks is on course to be made official and announced shortly. More NBA from @jakelfischer.bsky.social and me: tinyurl.com/5b4w9jws

item.title: Their $13.4 million trade exception (expires today) …
item.description: ‪Yossi Gozlan‬: The Wizards remain elite at using trade exceptions before they expire. Their $13.4 million trade exception (expires today) fits Deandre Ayton ($8.1 million) & Khris Middleton ($5.6 million) to give him exactly three years, $17.6 million. This allows them to create two new TPEs.

item.title: Khris Middleton signed a 3-year, $17.61 million deal. …
item.description: Michael Scotto: Khris Middleton signed a 3-year, $17.61 million deal. It’s fully guaranteed for the ‘26-27 season ($5.59 million), partially guaranteed for the ‘27-28 season ($909K guaranteed of $5.87 million), and non-guaranteed for the ‘28-29 season, as part of a sign-and-trade to the Wizards.

item.title: Six-team trade involving Mavs, Pistons, Clippers, Grizzlies, Bucks and Wizards made official
item.description: The Dallas Mavericks announced today their participation in a six-team trade involving the Detroit Pistons, L.A. Clippers, Memphis Grizzlies, Milwaukee Bucks and Washington Wizards. Dallas acquired forward Santi Aldama and the rights to forward Tarik Biberović from Memphis along with guard Marcus Sasser from Detroit. Dallas traded guard AJ Johnson, a top-20 protected 2030 first-round pick (via Golden State) and a 2029 second-round pick (via Houston) to Memphis. The Mavericks also sent forward K

item.title: Wizards General Manager Will Dawkins on Trae Young: …
item.description: Michael Scotto: Wizards General Manager Will Dawkins on Trae Young: “Trae made an immediate impact on our organization and the DMV when he arrived in January. Re-signing one of the league’s premier point guards was a top priority for us this offseason as we continue to build here in D.C.”

item.title: Trae Young: You about to get the best version of me🙏🏽
item.description: Trae Young: You about to get the best version of me🙏🏽

item.title: The NBA has informed the Utah Jazz that Keyonte George …
item.description: After reviewing the situation and relying on precedent, the NBA has informed the Utah Jazz that Keyonte George will not be able to coach during their Las Vegas Summer League game against the Washington Wizards on Thursday, league sources told the Deseret News. The Jazz were informed Wednesday that George, the same as any other active player, will not be allowed to take on any kind of official coaching role, in summer league or otherwise, even without compensation. George is not the first active

item.title: Anthony Davis is getting in a shooting workout here on …
item.description: Josh Robbins: Anthony Davis is getting in a shooting workout here on the Wizards' summer-league practice court. Tristan Vukčević is on the court, too, to get shots up.

item.title: Wizards owner Ted Leonsis, GM Will Dawkins, coach …
item.description: Ben Golliver: Wizards owner Ted Leonsis, GM Will Dawkins, coach Brian Keefe, Anthony Davis, Trae Young, Deandre Ayton and Alex Sarr all here to watch AJ Dybantsa’s Las Vegas Summer League debut

item.title: Another symbol of Anthony Davis' chapter in Los …
item.description: Another symbol of Anthony Davis' chapter in Los Angeles is officially gone. The Washington Wizards big man sold his Los Angeles home Thursday for a whopping $32,000,000.

item.title: “No player that we consider that’s been in a GOAT …
item.description: NBA Courtside: Paul Pierce says if LeBron James goes to the Sixers it takes him totally out the GOAT debate: “No player that we consider that’s been in a GOAT debate has been a fourth option. No, seriously. Like Jordan, even when he went to the Wizards, was still the first option. Kareem was second option. Worh Magic. Like when we start having these debates about the GOAT and we say, we’ll, you know, Philly won a championship, but he was fourth option. He has five rings but fourth and fifth opti

item.title: Rich Paul says Anthony Davis will play for the Wizards …
item.description: Legion Hoops: Rich Paul says Anthony Davis will play for the Wizards next season: “Yea, why wouldn’t he play for the Wizards?” (via Game Over, h/t @NBA__Courtside)

item.title: "Every time I play him, it's a great setting," …
item.description: Dybantsa led all players with 27 points and seven rebounds in 26 minutes to power Washington to a 92-88 win. Peterson finished with 24 points. "Every time I play him, it's a great setting," Dybantsa told ESPN afterward. "He got the best of me three times -- two in high school, one in college. I just wanted to come out on top, get a win first and foremost."

item.title: Bill Simmons on LeBron's free agency: 'The Cleveland thing is done'
item.description: Bill Simmons claimed that James going home to reunite with the Cleveland Cavaliers for the second time has already been determined. "The thing that happened to poor Golden State is, I think they thought they were getting LeBron and potentially [Anthony Davis] and now, it's pretty clear they were being used by leverage, as leverage, as LeBron goes back to Cleveland," the NBA pundit first said on the latest episode of his The Bill Simmons Podcast. When pressed by Joe House about the leverage he wa

item.title: Anthony Davis worked out earlier this morning at the …
item.description: Ohm Youngmisuk: Anthony Davis worked out earlier this morning at the Wizards practice facility setup and looked in great shape. Trae Young is in town as is Wizards owner Ted Leonsis for a night the franchise has waited years for.

item.title: Most points by a 1st overall pick in their summer …
item.description: ‪Oh No He Didn't‬: Most points by a 1st overall pick in their summer league debut: AJ Dybantsa and Blake Griffin - 27 points 😳

item.title: "After I got traded, like it just felt like this could …
item.description: ClutchPoints: "After I got traded, like it just felt like this could be my next home. ... When I heard there was a chance that I could come here, I was super excited [about] that possibility." Trae Young on his new contract extension with the Wizards.

item.title: “I’m ready to play tomorrow after seeing them play …
item.description: Ohm Youngmisuk: Trae Young on what he was thinking when he watched AJ Dybantsa and the young Wizards summer league team last night: “I’m ready to play tomorrow after seeing them play last night. When you see what they did last night gives you more excitement.”

item.title: “That ain’t nothing new. I mean, it isn’t the first …
item.description: That contract raised a lot of eyebrows around the league. Young made $46.4 million last season, and there was no trade market for him at the deadline — Atlanta essentially salary-dumped him to Washington. The expectation around the league was that Young would sign an extension, but at a considerably lower per-year number. His value wasn’t there. Instead, the Wizards gave him a raise. Young heard all the talk and shrugged it off. “That ain’t nothing new. I mean, it isn’t the first time all this h

item.title: “You have a mixture of both, that’s this makes great …
item.description: “You have a mixture of both, that’s this makes great team,” Young said. “I mean, you can’t have a team full of just older players that can’t run. You can’t have a team with just young players that don’t know the game, been in those experiences. So you have a mixture of both.” These kinds of press conferences rarely yield real insights or news, but one thing that was clear from this event is that Young is excited to be in Washington and with this team. “Will (Dawkins, Wizards GM) said it, that’s
